reason: Response A reads as the genuine Haiku: it has a more straightforward, informative register, packs in factual detail (Greek roots petros/ichor, the 1964 coinage) and reaches a conventional emotional conclusion without the heavily stylized literary affectation. Response B leans on punchy short sentences, anthropomorphizing flourishes ("Susurrus knows it is beautiful," "wears its beauty openly"), and an aphoristic structure that reads like a stronger model deliberately performing a "writerly" voice rather than Haiku's plainer expository style.
